evidence: All 7 platform adapters inherit BasePlatformAdapter and implement the same abstract interface (connect, disconnect, send, send_typing, get_chat_info)
All tool files use registry.register() at module level following a documented, consistent pattern
Uniform type strategy: only dataclass (38 instances), no TypedDict/NamedTuple/dataclass mixing
All tool call parsers follow the same ABC + @register_parser decorator pattern
sys.path.insert used in 4 of 7 platform adapters (telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp) but not in the other 3 (signal, email, homeassistant) — same directory, two import conventions
check_ha_requirements abbreviates the platform name while all 6 sibling adapters use full names (check_telegram_requirements, etc.)
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: sys.path.insert inconsistency across sibling platform adapters creates two import conventions in the same directory; check_ha_requirements naming outlier breaks the naming pattern established by all other adapters; tools/__init__.py is a 264-line re-export surface that is largely bypassed by actual consumers

convention_outlier.dimension_character: The codebase follows conventions with strong consistency in the areas that matter most: naming (snake_case throughout), type strategy (dataclass only), tool registration (registry.register pattern), and platform adapter structure (shared ABC with matching check_*_requirements functions). The few outliers are localized — a split in sys.path.insert usage among sibling platform adapters and one abbreviated function name — rather than systemic drift. The large tools/__init__.py barrel export is a minor convention outlier since it is rarely used by actual consumers.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: A developer can generally see a pattern in one area and trust it holds elsewhere. The tool registration, parser, and platform adapter patterns are all consistent and well-documented. The sys.path.insert split in gateway/platforms/ is the most notable inconsistency — it affects sibling modules in the same directory with no apparent rationale — pulling the score below 90. The check_ha_requirements naming outlier is minor but real. Overall this is solid with a few isolated rough edges, placing it near the 85 anchor.

issues.summary: 4 of 7 platform adapters use sys.path.insert while 3 import directly — inconsistent sibling convention
evidence: telegram.py, discord.py, slack.py, whatsapp.py all have sys.path.insert(0, str(_Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2])) before importing from gateway.config
signal.py, email.py, homeassistant.py import from gateway.config and gateway.platforms.base directly without sys.path manipulation
All 7 files are siblings in the same directory importing the same upstream modules
issues.suggestion: Remove the sys.path.insert calls from telegram.py, discord.py, slack.py, and whatsapp.py to match the direct-import convention used by signal.py, email.py, and homeassistant.py. The gateway package is already importable via normal Python path resolution when running through the entry points.

issues.summary: check_ha_requirements uses abbreviated name while all 6 other adapters use full platform name
evidence: All other adapters: check_telegram_requirements, check_discord_requirements, check_slack_requirements, check_whatsapp_requirements, check_signal_requirements, check_email_requirements
issues.suggestion: Rename check_ha_requirements to check_homeassistant_requirements to match the naming convention used by all other platform adapters. Update the 1-2 call sites accordingly.

issues.summary: tools/__init__.py is a 264-line re-export barrel that consumers largely bypass via direct imports
evidence: tools/__init__.py re-exports 60+ symbols from 15+ tool modules across 264 lines
Actual consumers (gateway/run.py, model_tools.py, cron/scheduler.py, tests) import directly from tools.<module> rather than through the barrel
The registry.register() pattern at module scope is the real tool discovery mechanism, making the barrel redundant
issues.suggestion: Slim tools/__init__.py down to minimal re-exports or just the package docstring. Since the registry pattern handles tool discovery and consumers already import directly from submodules, the large barrel adds maintenance burden without value.

root_causes: Platform adapters were added by different contributors at different times — earlier adapters (telegram, discord) used sys.path hacks, later ones (signal, email) used proper relative imports

add.description: All Python modules use snake_case for functions/variables and PascalCase for classes. No camelCase drift detected across any package boundary.
Every tool file in tools/ calls registry.register() at module level. model_tools.py triggers registration by importing all tool modules. This pattern is documented and followed consistently.
All 7 platform adapters inherit BasePlatformAdapter, implement the same abstract methods, and expose a module-level check_*_requirements() function. The structural contract is reliable.
All 38 domain data types use @dataclass. No mixed TypedDict/NamedTuple usage, eliminating one common source of convention drift.
All parser classes in environments/tool_call_parsers/ inherit from ToolCallParser ABC and use the @register_parser decorator. Adding a new parser follows an obvious pattern.

telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp adapters use sys.path.insert before importing gateway modules; signal, email, homeassistant import directly. Likely reflects different authorship periods — earlier adapters added the hack, later ones found it unnecessary.
